Simulation-calibrated ancestry calling: two-stage parameter search

Materials & Methods §2.4–2.5 + Results §3.1–3.2 — calibrate on simcross, validate on truth

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 4, 2026

Thesis. Ancestry-caller segmentation parameters (rrate for nNIL, rigidity for RTIGER) can be calibrated from cheap Broman simcross simulations where the donor mosaic is known truth, rather than tuned by eye on real data.

Method — a two-stage search (R/calibrate.R, driven by scripts/02_calibrate.R):

  1. Log sweep — score a coarse log-spaced grid against truth to bracket the optimum (sweep_calibrate → one shared nilHMM::caller_sweep fit + scoring).
  2. Golden-ratio refine — golden-section search inside the bracket on the continuous log10(parameter) to localize it (golden_refine).

The objective is donor-fragment Dice (reciprocal-overlap of called vs true introgression blocks) — it penalizes both missed donor and the spurious 1-marker blocks that over-fragmentation produces, unlike marker recall which saturates.

NoteCompute is separated from this note

scripts/02_calibrate.R runs the sim, calibration, and benchmark, writing summary CSVs to results/sim/ (gitignored). This note only loads and plots them. Regenerate with Rscript scripts/02_calibrate.R --generate.

suppressMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(ggplot2)
})
SIM <- here::here("results/sim")
f <- function(x) file.path(SIM, x)
have <- file.exists(f("calib_params.csv"))
if (have) {
  P <- fread(f("calib_params.csv"))
  par <- setNames(P$value, P$key)
  nnil_sw <- fread(f("nnil_rrate_sweep.csv"))
  rtig_sw <- fread(f("rtiger_rigidity_sweep.csv"))
  nnil_rf <- fread(f("nnil_rrate_refine.csv"))
  rtig_rf <- fread(f("rtiger_rigidity_refine.csv"))
  bench <- fread(f("benchmark.csv"))
  frag <- fread(f("frag_sizes.csv"))
  geno <- fread(f("geno_fractions.csv"))
}

1. The simulation and its truth (§2.4)

BC2S2, n = 1500, simcross on the maize consensus map (~1783 cM, Stahl interference m = 10 / p = 0), 50 000 markers, degraded to the skim regime (~0.43× / ~72% missing). Truth = the donor mosaic. The simulated genome composition should match the single-locus (Mendelian) expectation:

data.table(
  metric = c("REF", "HET", "ALT", "donor dosage"),
  simulated = round(par[c("REF_truth", "HET_truth", "ALT_truth", "dosage_truth")], 4),
  expected = round(par[c("REF_expect", "HET_expect", "ALT_expect", "dosage_expect")], 4)
)
         metric simulated expected
         <char>     <num>    <num>
1:          REF    0.8413   0.8438
2:          HET    0.0630   0.0625
3:          ALT    0.0958   0.0938
4: donor dosage    0.1272   0.1250

2. Two-stage calibration

For each caller the coarse log sweep (line) brackets the optimum, and the golden-ratio refine (points) homes in; the dashed line is the refined parameter carried to the benchmark.

calib_panel <- function(sweep, refine, star, xlab, xtrans) {
  ggplot(sweep, aes(value)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = donor_frag_dice, colour = "donor-fragment Dice")) +
    geom_line(aes(y = donor_frag_FDR, colour = "donor-fragment FDR")) +
    geom_point(data = refine, aes(value, objective, colour = "golden probes"), size = 1.6) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = star, linetype = "dashed") +
    xtrans +
    labs(x = xlab, y = "score", colour = NULL) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")
}
p_nnil <- calib_panel(nnil_sw, nnil_rf, par["rrate_star"], "nNIL rrate", scale_x_log10()) +
  ggtitle(sprintf("nNIL — refined rrate = %.2g", par["rrate_star"]))
p_rtig <- calib_panel(
  rtig_sw, rtig_rf, par["rigidity_star"], "RTIGER rigidity",
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2")
) +
  ggtitle(sprintf("RTIGER — refined rigidity = %d", as.integer(par["rigidity_star"])))
patchwork_ok <- requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)
if (patchwork_ok) {
  print(patchwork::wrap_plots(p_nnil, p_rtig))
} else {
  print(p_nnil)
  print(p_rtig)
}
# The log-sweep curves (Dice peaks then over-fragments as FDR/breakpoints climb).
show <- function(s, pcol) {
  s[, .(
    value = signif(value, 3), donor_frag_dice = round(donor_frag_dice, 3),
    donor_frag_FDR = round(donor_frag_FDR, 3), donor_marker_recall = round(donor_marker_recall, 3),
    n_breakpoints, truth_bp, ks = round(ks_fragsize, 3)
  )]
}
cat("nNIL rrate log sweep:\n")
nNIL rrate log sweep:
print(show(nnil_sw))
       value donor_frag_dice donor_frag_FDR donor_marker_recall n_breakpoints

3. Benchmark at the calibrated parameters (§2.5, §3.2)

Each caller run at its refined optimum on a held-out set, scored vs truth:

bench[]
        caller       param marker_macro_dice donor_marker_dice
        <char>       <num>             <num>             <num>
1:   Skim-nNIL 0.001096669             0.995             0.998
2: Skim-RTIGER 2.000000000             0.996             0.998
   donor_marker_recall donor_frag_dice donor_frag_FDR ks_fragsize breakpoints
                 <num>           <num>          <num>       <num>       <int>
1:               0.996           0.963          0.015       0.022        4791
2:               0.997           0.964          0.013       0.021        4879
   breakpoints_truth
               <int>
1:              5199
2:              5199

4. Fragment-size fit (supplementary)

Donor-block size distributions vs the BC2S2 truth (the calibrated callers should track the truth’s fragment sizes; KS in the benchmark table).

ggplot(frag, aes(mb, colour = src)) +
  stat_ecdf() +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "donor block size (Mb)", y = "ECDF", title = "Fragment-size fit vs truth")

5. Genotype-frequency vs the one-locus expectation (supplementary)

ggplot(melt(geno[, .(src, HET, ALT)], id.vars = "src"), aes(src, value)) +
  geom_violin(scale = "width") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_hline(data = data.table(
    variable = c("HET", "ALT"), y = c(par["HET_expect"], par["ALT_expect"])
  ), aes(yintercept = y), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "genome fraction", title = "Genotype frequency vs one-locus expectation (dashed)") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

Next steps

  • Add the BrB regime and carry the per-source calibrated parameters to 03-source-method-comparison.qmd.
  • Sharpen the objective’s soft plateau near the optimum with a KS or breakpoint-match tie-breaker. ```
